stories.title: CPSC Recall — Sam's Club Member's Mark Children's Pajamas Fail Flammability Standards [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: RECALL: Sam's Club Member's Mark children's pajama sets (sizes 4/5–14/16) fail flammability standards. ~18,000 units sold Dec–Jan for ~$14. Stop use immediately. Return to any Sam's Club for full refund. 🔥 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Colorado parents: if your child wears Member's Mark Valentine-print pajamas from Sam's Club, they're recalled for burn risk. Sizes XS–XL (4/5–14/16). Stop use. Full refund at Sam's Club. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you picked up children's pajamas from Sam's Club over the winter, take a minute to check the label.

On April 2, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission announced a recall of approximately 18,000 Sam's Club "Member's Mark Valentine SS Notch Collar" children's pajama sets. These are the short-sleeve button-up pajama tops with a front pocket — they came in pink solid, pink hearts, and cream bows prints, in sizes XS (4/5) through XL (14/16). The problem: they fail mandatory federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear, which means they create a burn risk that the law specifically requires sleepwear to prevent.

They were sold on Samsclub.com between December 2025 and January 2026 for around $14. If you have them, stop using them now. Here's what to do: return them to any local Sam's Club for a full refund, or if you'd rather dispose of them, cut the pajamas in half and email a photo to Supersam@Samsclub.com for your refund.

This recall is fresh — issued just two days ago — so there's a good chance these are still in rotation in a lot of families' pajama drawers. The holiday/Valentine color palette makes them recognizable. Check your kids' drawers tonight.

Did you buy these? Share this post so other Sam's Club families can check too.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM MT

```
RECALL: Sam's Club Member's Mark children's pajama sets (Valentine-print, sizes 4/5–14/16) fail flammability standards. Return for full refund or dispose. Issued April 2. 🔥
```

article.headline: Sam's Club Recalls Member's Mark Children's Pajamas for Burn Hazard — Check Your Drawers Now

stories.title: CPSC Urgent Warning — Fasando Baby Lounger Linked to Infant Death [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: URGENT: CPSC warns to immediately stop using Fasando baby loungers. A 2-month-old died after being placed on an adult bed in one. ~6,200 units sold Amazon 2024–25. No recall — seller won't cooperate. Destroy and dispose. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: The Fasando baby lounger looks comfortable. CPSC says it's dangerous — a baby died using one on an adult bed. If you have one: remove the foam padding, cut the cover in half, dispose. Do not donate or resell. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you have a Fasando baby lounger, stop using it right now — and please share this post.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has issued an urgent warning about Fasando brand baby loungers after a 2-month-old infant died after being placed to sleep in one on an adult bed. This is not a formal recall — the Chinese seller has refused to cooperate with CPSC, which means the product is still potentially available on resale markets, in thrift stores, or from families passing baby gear along. If you have one, it needs to be destroyed.

Here's what CPSC says to do: stop using it immediately. Remove the foam padding from inside. Cut the cover in half. Then dispose of it — do not donate it, do not sell it, do not pass it along. Approximately 6,200 units were sold on Amazon between August 2024 and July 2025 for around $35.

The reason CPSC is asking parents to destroy rather than return is because there's no cooperation from the seller. The only way to get these out of circulation is for every parent who has one to take action directly.

Baby loungers in general have been a product category with safety concerns — always check CPSC.gov before purchasing any infant sleep or rest product. If you're unsure whether your lounger is a Fasando brand, check the Amazon order history from 2024–2025.

Does your family have a baby lounger you're not sure about? Share this so other parents can check.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM MT

```
URGENT: Fasando baby lounger — infant death reported. CPSC says stop use immediately, remove foam padding, cut cover in half, dispose. Do NOT donate or resell. ~6,200 units sold on Amazon 2024–25.
```

article.headline: CPSC Issues Urgent Warning on Fasando Baby Loungers After Infant Death — No Recall Issued Yet

stories.title: COS Parks Budget Cuts — What Families Will Actually Notice This Summer [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: COS families: 10 park restrooms closing this summer. Broken playground equipment may be removed, not repaired. Julie Penrose Fountain won't run. Parks budget cut $3.1M. Check before you pack the diaper bag. 🌳 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Before you plan your park days this summer, here's what Colorado Springs families need to know about what's changing — and what's not.

Colorado Springs Parks, Recreation, and Cultural Services is working with a $3.1 million smaller budget in 2026 after lower-than-expected sales tax revenue. Here's the practical impact for families:

🚻 10 neighborhood park restrooms are closing — that's 25% of permanent restrooms across the city. No portable restrooms will be placed in neighborhood parks except for organized sports events and permitted activities.

🛝 Playground repairs are being cut in half. If a piece of equipment breaks, the city may remove it instead of repairing it. Surfacing material budgets are also cut 50%.

⛲ Julie Penrose Fountain at America the Beautiful Park will not operate this summer. The staff who maintained it have been reassigned.

🏢 Meadows Park Community Center has been permanently closed since October 2025.

What IS still happening: the city's TOPS (Trails, Open Space, Parks) program is proceeding with 2026 projects, including a 210-foot pedestrian bridge over Sand Creek that will connect the Rock Island Trail east-west.

The practical advice: if you're planning a park day with young kids, check whether the restrooms at your destination will be open this summer. The city has not yet published a full list of the 10 closing locations, so when in doubt, plan for no restroom access.

Which parks do your kids visit most? We'd love to help families know what to expect.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 12:00 PM MT

```
COS parks update for 2026: 10 restrooms closing, playground repairs cut in half, Julie Penrose Fountain not operating. Budget cut $3.1M. Plan your park days accordingly. 🌳
```

article.headline: COS Parks Budget Cuts: 10 Restrooms Closing, Playgrounds Won't Get Repaired — What Families Need to Know for Summer 2026

stories.title: Easter Weekend + Switchbacks Home Opener — COS Family Weekend Preview [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Easter Sunday in Colorado Springs: Cheyenne Mountain Resort egg hunt + wildlife (seatings at 10am/12:30pm/3pm). Spruce Mountain Ranch Easter Bunny + horse rides + crafts (10:30am–1pm). Fun for all ages. 🐣 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Happy Easter weekend, Colorado Springs families. We've got options for you today and tomorrow.

TODAY (Saturday, April 4): The Colorado Springs Switchbacks FC host Lexington SC at Weidner Field in downtown Colorado Springs. Kickoff is at 3 PM, gates open at 2 PM, and tickets start at $23. The Switchbacks are riding some momentum after advancing in the US Open Cup with a penalty kick win on Thursday — great energy for a home game. Weidner Field is a fun outdoor stadium experience for families.

TOMORROW (Easter Sunday, April 5): Two very different options depending on your vibe:

🐣 Cheyenne Mountain Resort: Join them for an Easter morning with a live rabbit meet-and-greet, an interactive wildlife presentation, and a scenic egg hunt while hiking the property. Seatings are at 10 AM, 12:30 PM, and 3 PM.

🐴 Spruce Mountain Ranch: Easter Brunch with a full experience — the Easter Bunny, professional photos, crafts, an egg hunt, horse carriage rides, and live entertainment. Runs 10:30 AM–1 PM.

Two different price points, two different vibes. The Resort is more of a nature experience; the Ranch leans into the full Easter pageantry. Both are wonderful options for families.

What does your family have planned for Easter this year?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM MT

```
Easter weekend in COS: Switchbacks home game today at 3pm, Cheyenne Mountain Resort egg hunt + wildlife Sunday (10am/12:30pm/3pm), and Spruce Mountain Ranch Easter Brunch Sunday (10:30am–1pm). 🐣
```

article.headline: Easter Weekend Family Guide: Switchbacks Home Game, Cheyenne Mountain Resort Egg Hunt, and Spruce Mountain Ranch Brunch

stories.title: AAP Drops the '2-Hour Rule' — New Screen Time Policy Is a Big Shift [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: AAP (Jan 2026): the 2-hour screen time rule is out. New policy focuses on content quality and family culture, not clock-watching. Introduces the '5 C's' framework. Guilt-counting iPad minutes is over. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you've been counting iPad minutes and feeling guilty about your kids' screen time, the American Academy of Pediatrics has something to tell you: that approach is officially outdated.

In January 2026, the AAP published a landmark new policy statement in the journal Pediatrics called "Digital Ecosystems, Children, and Adolescents." The headline change: they are moving away from strict hourly screen time limits (the old "no more than 2 hours per day for ages 2 to 5" rule) in favor of a much more nuanced framework.

The new approach is built around the "5 C's":

📱 **Child** — your individual child's needs, temperament, and developmental stage
📺 **Content** — what are they actually watching or doing?
😴 **Calming** — are screens being used as a soothing tool? (The AAP wants families to be thoughtful about this)
⚽ **Crowding out** — is screen time displacing sleep, physical activity, or family connection?
💬 **Communication** — how does your family talk about and navigate media together?

Instead of enforced time limits, the AAP now recommends a Family Media Plan — a household agreement that reflects your family's specific values, routines, and child's needs. Device-free bedrooms and mealtimes are still strongly recommended.

Practically, this means: stop guilt-counting minutes and start asking better questions. Is what they're watching high quality? Is it displacing something important? Are we talking about it as a family?

What does your family's screen time routine look like? Has this policy shift changed how you think about it?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:00 PM MT

```
AAP update (Jan 2026): the 2-hour screen time rule is out. New "Digital Ecosystems" policy introduces the 5 C's framework — focus on content quality and family culture, not clock-watching.
```

article.headline: The AAP Just Retired the 2-Hour Screen Time Rule — Here Is What the New Policy Actually Says
